mentions.note: Favorite of the show, up 239% in 2025; memory chip maker with 30-year history of multi-year rallies (2013 +200%, 2014 +60%). Data center voracious demand may sustain run. Take profits on strength.
Memory chip shortage benefiting supplier; up 10% in one session on momentum buying and short covering. Supply constraints will persist until capital equipment makers catch up.
Data storage play with slightly more complication than WDC/Seagate; moving up with semiconductor capex companies.
Memory chip maker up 18% YTD; benefiting from data center demand for storage chips in short supply.
Announced $100B New York fab for memory chip production; critical shortage persists; stock up 27% YTD; data-center demand outpaced supply and will continue to grow.
Memory maker up huge; printing money but in year 2 of shortage; first sniff of supply catch-up will crash this; take profits significantly now.
Memory/storage leader, strong 2025/2026 on genuine shortage; supply will eventually catch up, first sniff of waning demand crashes these stocks—take significant profits.

Memory chip prices up 400–500% on AI demand explosion; parabolic 111%+ annual move unstoppable but historically risky; breaking ground $100B fab (2030 production); buy on pullback, don't chase parabolic.
Up parabolic on 400-500% memory price spike driven by AI demand; breaking ground on $100B fab (2030 production); no peak in sight but historically cycle-prone.
Storage play up 39% YTD; memory shortage supporting extended rally; devices like gasoline—no price resistance due to AI data center demand; Cramer sees further upside even at current levels.
Memory/storage play; endless buying in last year due to data center shortage surge. Shortage-driven rally continues.
Memory chip maker in short supply; data center demand driving shortages.

CEO Sanjay Mehrotra announced $100B foundry in New York (2030), new fab Idaho (H2 2027), new fab Singapore (H2 2028); multi-year production expansion guarantees sustained capex orders for equipment makers.
Memory/storage shortage creating absurd pricing power; CEO Sanjay Mehrotra breaking ground on new foundries; shortage lasts years.
#4 January performer, up 45%. Led by Sanjay Mehrotra (founder bloodlines); memory play riding AI capex wave; tripled in 2025.
Memory chip stock flying too high; avoid chasing; better memory names may bounce from depressed levels.
One of four critical memory makers facing severe undersupply; AI data needs insatiable; ration product to customers; memory prices can skyrocket without demand destruction.
Memory chip supplier caught in indiscriminate program sell-off. Cramer skeptical of bearish narratives; fundamentals strong despite AI hardware cooling.
Memory stocks brutally sold on South Korea contagion (SK Hynix -11.5%) and margin calls from Korean investors; spillover hit hard but fundamentals unchanged.
Memory play benefiting from 'they' buying signal in Seoul. Down most of week but poised to rebound on AI data center demand and positive trading desk momentum.
Semiconductor beneficiary on oil reversal and data center demand recovery.
Memory shortage / data center infrastructure build tailwind; HPE commentary validates multi-year memory constraints.
Memory supplier; theme intact but valuation stretched. Buyable on significant pullback if oil shock triggers broader selloff.
Caller Phil: After gigantic valuation move (market cap ~$500B), stock digesting gains. Let it churn. Do not buy here; wait for pullback below $18 for more meaningful entry.
Crushed on unverified Google memory-killer claim; stock had parabolic run so pullback justified. Google down when announced (poor sign for thesis). Memory stocks need more cooling before buy signal.

Memory producer up 18-57% Q1, only one adding capacity at scale; still sold out; reported incredible March quarter but stock cratered 461→321; Cramer bullish on 6-quarter boom cycle but notes easy money made.
Memory/storage chip maker; AI capex and data center demand.
Memory winner; ramping on hyperscaler demand for AI data centers; critical component shortage early prevented by Nvidia foresight.
Memory maker; up 41% since bottom but hasn't retaken mid-March high. Strong quarter became negative catalyst (overheated consensus). Keep one eye on exit; memory is boom-bust.
Memory chip maker benefiting from years-long capacity build-out lag; AI demand insatiable.
Memory and storage stocks ramping; up 5% today. Essential server components in short supply.
YoY +597% return; data-center and AI memory tailwind; demonstrates wealth creation vs index mediocrity.
Semiconductor memory manufacturer; shortage of memory and rising component costs driving demand.
Memory/storage hardware benefits from AI infrastructure capex boom. Long-term winner for disciplined holders.
Cheapest memory stock after 777% rally; data center demand endless; pricing improves as supply stays constrained; headed to $1,000; currently just 6x earnings.
Ridiculously huge gains in recent period; memory chip supplier central to AI infrastructure buildout; data-center demand driving.
Data center memory play riding overextended rally; part of group correction; need to let stocks 'churn and burn' before rolling again; selling pressure likely to persist on rebounds.
Memory chip player not overvalued despite sector strength and component shortages.
Memory stock at <12x earnings (vs. WDC at 46x); only worthwhile data center opportunity after extreme valuations elsewhere. Buy gingerly, add on 2–3% further decline.

Market cap topped $1 trillion on high-bandwidth memory strength for AI data centers; boom appears unrelenting vs. historical memory cyclicality; analysts view remaining upside even at current valuations.
HBM shortage + shallow float (30% in index/ETFs) + no new capacity = massive run from $500B to $1T in 48 days; UBS $1,625 PT tempting but Cramer won't chase into boom-bust cycle risk.

Hit $1T market cap in 48 days; HBM shortage acute, but historical boom-bust cycle warns. UBS PT $1,625. At $928 Cramer declines to buy despite upside; new capacity coming will eventually crush margins; willing to miss upside rather than walk into buzzsaw.
On track to earn over $100 per share next year; insanely profitable; real earnings power; not a dot-com speculative play.

Semiconductor maker appeared ridiculously expensive at mid-$80s when earning $1/share FY2024. Now projected $100+/share FY2027, trading $1,035, still only 10x earnings. Massively undervalued before boom recogni.
DRAM shortage driving 266% gain YTD; price target raises continue; can't make chips fast enough; part of endless shortage play.
